prompt: WRITE A SPEC ONLY (no build). Use the spec-writer skill (Skill tool: appydave:spec-writer). REQUIREMENT — 'Instrument-then-harden the Dark Factory dispatch loop' (factory-operations telemetry). Read these sources as the input: backlog/2026-06-08-stability-selflearning-lanes.md (the #1 section) + memory files at ~/.claude/projects/-Users-davidcruwys-dev-ad-apps-dark-factory/memory/{factory-operations-telemetry.md, dispatch-loop-robustness.md, observability-and-balanced-growth.md}. KEY PRINCIPLE the spec must center on: OBSERVE THE LOOP EXTERNALLY — the worker (Swagger) is the unreliable part; it skips its own bookkeeping (EVIDENCE: on 2026-06-08, 4 jobs moved to done/ but wrote NO run record and NO handback). So telemetry must be emitted by things OUTSIDE the worker: the dispatcher (engine/Marshall) emits 'dispatched'; claim-next + done/ appearance give 'claimed'/'done'; the reaper gives 'teardown'; derived flags computed externally = handback_written? run_record_written? teardown_clean? duration? stray_running?. AppyCtrl detects orphan tmux windows / stray processes ('left hanging'). Placement: lifecycle events -> Switchboard durable event log; orphan/hang detection -> AppyCtrl; query + visualise ('what works / what doesn't', failure counts, rates) -> Watchtower. The spec must define: the event schema, the metrics (handback-success rate, clean-teardown rate, orphan count, durations, FAILURE COUNTS), the smallest first step (emit dispatcher+reaper events to Switchboard to get handback-written-rate + clean-teardown-rate immediately), testable acceptance criteria, NFRs, and OPEN PO DECISIONS surfaced. Build-technique-agnostic; building is LATER. WRITE the spec to backlog/specs/stability-1-instrument-loop-spec.md. Then complete run-next-workflow bookkeeping (run record + done/ + reports/ handback — DO NOT skip the handback; it has been getting botched).
